mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate, directly increasing the present value of expected equity cash flows and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 4.5% within a single month, it signals a rapid deterioration in labor market conditions that triggers recession fears, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises above 4.5% in a single month
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the threshold crossing fails to predict a statistically significant drop in forward returns (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous contraction in industrial production and a sharp decline in retail sales indicates a synchronized demand-supply mismatch that erodes corporate profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint decline in INDPRO and RSAFS does not produce a statistically significant negative correlation with 60-day forward SPX returns (Rank IC > -0.02), or forward returns remain positive during concurrent contractions.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rapid monetary tightening, an increase in the federal funds rate combined with rising CPI inflation compresses real yields and triggers equity risk-off positioning, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS increases by > 0.25pp in a single month
failure_condition: Forward SPX returns are not significantly lower during tightening episodes compared to neutral periods, or the signal fails to predict declines when CPI remains below 3.0%.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month increase in new housing starts reflects resilient consumer confidence and credit availability, supporting forward-looking equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in rising housing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the industrial production index falls below 98.0, it signals a severe supply-side bottleneck reversal or demand collapse that disrupts corporate earnings guidance, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns do not decline significantly when INDPRO breaches the 98.0 threshold, or the signal generates false positives in > 40% of out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate concurrent with a federal funds rate above 3.0% indicates that the central bank is prioritizing inflation control over employment support, triggering a risk-off equity repricing and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition of rising UNRATE and FEDFUNDS > 3.0% fails to predict a statistically significant decline in 60-day forward SPX returns (Rank IC > -0.02), or equity valuations expand despite the dual pressure.

mechanism_narrative: During a late-cycle expansion characterized by peak payroll employment, a subsequent month-over-month decline in retail sales signals consumer exhaustion, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as earnings revisions turn sharply negative.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines for two consecutive months
failure_condition: Forward SPX returns remain positive or statistically indistinguishable from baseline when RSAFS declines consecutively during peak employment periods, or the signal fails to capture earnings revision downturns.
